subject: Joint Distributions
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be random variables with common range $\{1, 2\}$ and such that $\Pr(X = 1) = 0.7$, $\Pr(X = 2) = 0.3$, $\Pr(Y = 1) = 0.4$, $\Pr(Y = 2) = 0.6$, and $\Pr(X = 1, Y = 1) = 0.2$. (a) Find the joint probability mass function $p_{XY}(x, y)$. (b) Find the joint cumulative distribution function $F_{XY}(x, y)$.
answer: (a) We have $X \backslash Y$: row 1: $0.2, 0.5$ with $p_X(x) = 0.7$; row 2: $0.2, 0.1$ with $p_X(x) = 0.3$; $p_Y(y) = 0.4, 0.6$, total 1. (b) We have $F_{XY}(x, y) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 1 \text{ or } y < 1 \end{cases}$
explanation: Answer: (a) We have $X \backslash Y$: row 1: $0.2, 0.5$ with $p_X(x) = 0.7$; row 2: $0.2, 0.1$ with $p_X(x) = 0.3$; $p_Y(y) = 0.4, 0.6$, total 1. (b) We have $F_{XY}(x, y) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 1 \text{ or } y < 1 \end{cases}$
